text.title: Sprint Goal One Liner Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Sprint Goal One Liner Template application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Output sentence MUST fill all five slots: sprint-number, verb, user/system, measurable benefit, surface, out-of-scope. Empty or '?' in any slot rejects the goal.
rule.rationale: Sprint-goal template research — anchors Sprint Goal One Liner Template rule `five-slots-filled`.

rule.statement: The verb slot MUST express a state change (reduce, enable, ship, eliminate), not a task action (refactor, draft, investigate).
rule.rationale: Outcome-vs-output discipline — anchors Sprint Goal One Liner Template rule `verb-must-be-outcome`.

rule.statement: The 'so that' clause MUST contain a number + threshold + window, not adjectives. 'Users are happier' fails; '≥65% signup completion on staging cohort within sprint' passes.
rule.rationale: Goal-Question-Metric — anchors Sprint Goal One Liner Template rule `benefit-is-measurable`.

rule.statement: The full sentence MUST be ≤30 words and pass the recitability test: each team member can paraphrase it without notes by day 2.
rule.rationale: Cognitive-load research — anchors Sprint Goal One Liner Template rule `recitable-at-standup`.

rule.statement: The deferring clause MUST name a concrete out-of-scope item the team WAS tempted to add. If nothing is being deferred, the sprint is not focused.
rule.rationale: Scope-discipline practice — anchors Sprint Goal One Liner Template rule `explicit-out-of-scope`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
